channel.title: Democratic Integrity on Asymmetric Intelligence
channel.description: Weekly early-warning intelligence on democratic backsliding, institutional erosion, and state capture — tracking V-Dem and Freedom House signals before annual indices catch up. Published every Monday.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 27 April 2026
item.description: United States has crossed threshold into electoral democracy status with structural risks to multiple democratic pillars

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 20 April 2026
item.description: Hungary exemplifies autocratisation from within via institutional capture

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: US faces rapid institutional decay with vdem_ert_active across multiple pillars

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 13 April 2026
item.description: US institutional capture accelerates autocratization risking FIMI vulnerability

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 6 April 2026
item.description: Hungary votes on April 12 with Tisza polling 19–23 points ahead of Fidesz — the most consequential EU electoral test in a decade — while Benin holds a closed presidential contest on the same day and the US executive-SCOTUS confrontation enters a new phase.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 1 April 2026
item.description: Hungary enters the final 11 days with aggregate polling at 50.8% for Tisza — a potential end to 16 years of illiberal rule — while the US Supreme Court heard oral arguments testing whether the 14th Amendment citizenship clause can be reinterpreted by executive order alone.

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — 30 March 2026
item.description: Tisza leads Fidesz by 23 points with 13 days to Hungary's April 12 election; Georgia's new March 2026 law package completes its civil society capture architecture; and the Philippines enters the heatmap as a new Rapid Decay entry after CIVICUS adds it to its Watchlist at Repressed rating.
